name: Aditi
description: Aditi, in Hindu mythology, is a primordial goddess representing the infinite, the boundless, and the mother of all gods. She is the personification of the sky, consciousness, the past, the future, and fertility. Born from Daksha and Asikni, she is often depicted as the mother of the Adityas, a group of solar deities including Varuna, Mitra, Aryaman, Bhaga, Amsa, and Daksha (in some accounts). Her name literally means 'unbound' or 'limitless,' reflecting her role as the cosmic womb from which the universe and the gods emerged. Aditi is a powerful and benevolent figure, associated with freedom, safety, and forgiveness. She is considered the protector of her devotees, offering refuge from suffering and granting boons. Her influence extends to the realms of creation, preservation, and destruction, as she embodies the cyclical nature of existence. She is revered as the great mother goddess, the source of all life and the foundation of cosmic order. While not always directly involved in dramatic narratives, her presence is fundamental to the overall structure and function of the Hindu pantheon, providing the divine lineage and the underlying principle of cosmic harmony. She is a symbol of hope, abundance, and the infinite potential of the universe. Aditi's role is crucial in maintaining the balance between the divine and the mortal realms, ensuring the continuation of life and the upholding of dharma.
subtitle: The Boundless Mother of the Gods

keyMyths.title: Birth of the Adityas
keyMyths.description: Aditi, through her divine power, gave birth to the Adityas, the solar deities who embody various aspects of cosmic order and righteousness. These gods play crucial roles in maintaining balance and upholding dharma in the universe.

keyMyths.title: Aditi's Forgiveness
keyMyths.description: Indra, after committing a grave sin, sought refuge and forgiveness from his mother, Aditi. Her boundless compassion and ability to absolve sins highlight her role as a source of redemption.

keyMyths.title: The Cosmic Womb
keyMyths.description: Aditi is often described as the cosmic womb from which the universe and all its beings originated. This emphasizes her role as the primordial source of creation and the foundation of existence.

sources.author: F. Max Müller
sources.description: Translation and analysis of the Rigveda hymns.
sources.text: Translation and analysis of the Rigveda hymns.
